        LLL Senior Living Spaces · 2026
      
      Welcome to61 Victoria
      
        We are a Stellenbosch University house committed to quality education
        and equipping Makupula Secondary School learners with the tools
        to access higher education.
      
      
        SDG 4Our focus
        3Concepts
        2+Years running
      
    
  

  
  
    
      
        
        Partner: Makupula Secondary High School
      
      
        
        Stellenbosch University
      
      
        
        February – August 2026
      
    
  

  
  
    
      
        About this ePortfolio
        Our story, in your hands
        
          Every year, 61 Victoria House takes on a social impact project rooted in
          the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. In 2026, we are committed to
          SDG 4 — Quality Education, working directly with learners at Makupula
          Secondary High School who dream of studying further but lack access to guidance.
        
        
          This ePortfolio documents what we have done, who joined us, and what we learned
          along the way. Use the links below — or the navigation above — to explore each
          section of our work.

                                                                            SDG 4 — Quality Education
                                                                              The Project
                                                                              Our challenge, our plan, and the two concepts we have completed so far.
                                                                              
                                                                              
                                                                              
                                                                              
                                                                              
                                                                                
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  House profile
                                                                                    Who we are
                                                                                      
                                                                                        House61 Victoria
                                                                                          ClusterSenior Living Spaces – LLL Houses
                                                                                            Community partnerMakupula Secondary High School
                                                                                              Project statusContinuing since 2024/25
                                                                                                Focus areaUniversity readiness & mentorship
                                                                                                  Social impact leader61 Victoria House
                                                                                                    
                                                                                                    
                                                                                                    
                                                                                                    Why we do this
                                                                                                      The challenge we are addressing
                                                                                                        
                                                                                                          4
                                                                                                            
                                                                                                              SDG 4 — Quality Education
                                                                                                              Ensure inclusive and equitable quality education and promote lifelong learning opportunities for all.
                                                                                                              
                                                                                                              
                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                Many learners at Makupula Secondary lack structured guidance on university applications,
                                                                                                              available opportunities, and academic expectations for tertiary study. 61 Victoria bridges
                                                                                                              this gap by drawing on our lived experience to mentor top-performing students and equip
                                                                                                              them with the skills and confidence to pursue higher education.
                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                Implementation plan
                                                                                                                Three phases of action
                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                01
                                                                                                                February – March 2026
                                                                                                                Preparation
                                                                                                                Meeting school leadership to align goals and schedules, preparing printed materials and workshop templates, and liaising with SU structures.
                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                02
                                                                                                                April – July 2026
                                                                                                                Implementation
                                                                                                                Rolling out all three solution concepts—the SU open day, the university applications session, and the CV & professional identity workshop.
                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                03
                                                                                                                August 2026
                                                                                                                Reflection & sustainability
                                                                                                                Collecting feedback, capturing outcomes, and presenting findings to the LLL cohort to secure the programme's long-term future.
          
        

        

        
        What we have done
        Concepts completed
        Two of our three planned concepts have been successfully delivered. Photos from each are shown below.

        

          
          
            
              1
              
                
                Completed
              
              Stellenbosch University Open Day
            
            
              We took top-performing Makupula learners on a guided visit to Stellenbosch University, exposing them to diverse faculties, campus life, and career paths. The experience made tertiary study feel tangible and within reach for every learner who attended.
             
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                   
                  

          
          

          
          
            
              2
              
                
                Completed
              
              University applications & degree requirements session
            
            
              We guided learners through the SU application process, covering degree requirements, NSFAS and bursary applications, and residency options — giving students a concrete roadmap and the confidence to apply.

      House Dinner Conversations
      Our Dinner Guests
      The people who joined our dinner conversations and enriched our thinking on quality education and access to higher learning.
    
  

  
    
      

        Dinner conversations
        Guests who joined us
        
          Our house dinners created a space for honest, in-depth conversations about
          education inequality, access, and the role university students can play in
          uplifting their communities. We were honoured to host these guests.
        

        
  
  
  
  
  
  FS
  
  Fatima Shabodien
  Independent Development Consultant
  Fatima Shabodien is a South African feminist activist and academic whose work focuses on gender justice, social inequality, and the lasting impacts of apartheid. She began her activism during the late apartheid period and has since played an important role in advancing intersectional feminist thought in South Africa. Her work highlights how race, class, and gender intersect, particularly in shaping the experiences of Black women in the post-apartheid era.
  
  "Move beyond perfomartive activism and engage in conversations that will promote justice and equality within our communities."

  Voices from 61 Victoria
  Housemate Reflections
  Personal reflections from the members of 61 Victoria on what this project has meant, what was learned, and what we carry forward.
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  Housemate reflections
  In our own words
  
  Each member of 61 Victoria came to this project with a different background and
perspective. These are our honest reflections on what it meant to work alongside
the learners of Makupula Secondary High School.

  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  "The Makupula Secondary School project has been a meaningful and eye-opening experience, as through both the Stellenbosch University Open Day and our visit to Makupula Secondary School, I gained a deeper understanding of how important access to information, exposure, and guidance is for learners preparing for university. At the Open Day, I saw how powerful exposure can be, as for many learners it was their first time on a university campus, which helped make higher education feel more real and attainable, and I realised how unfamiliar and intimidating these spaces can feel, and how much confidence can grow simply through guided exposure and open conversations. At Makupula, I learned the value of patience and clear communication in mentorship, as many learners are capable but lack structured support around applications, funding, and study options, and this experience showed me how important it is to create a space where learners feel supported and comfortable asking questions."

  
  SN
  
  Sikhanyiso Nkosi
  61 Victoria, 2026
  
  
  
  
  
  
  "Working on the Makupula Secondary School project has been an enriching experience that highlighted for me how uneven access to guidance and information can shape learners’ educational journeys. Being part of the SU Open Day allowed me to see how powerful it is for learners to step into a university setting, as it shifts higher education from something abstract into something tangible, while also revealing how uncertain many students feel when encountering such environments for the first time. Our follow-up visit to Makupula Secondary School further reinforced this, as engaging directly with learners showed me that many have strong aspirations but often lack clarity on the practical steps needed to pursue them, particularly around applications, funding, and available programmes. Overall, the project made me more aware of how meaningful consistent engagement and accessible guidance can be in helping learners better understand their options and feel more confident about their futures after matric."

  
  TK
  
  Tshulani Khosa
  61 Victoria, 2026
  
  
  
  
  
  
  "Working with Makupula Secondary School and continuing the relationship established by previous LLL houses with the same SDG has shown the importance of sustainability of any project - but particularly those which contribute to the shaping of young minds and their access to fair and reliant mentorship. Our sessions thus far have shown how important it is to share resources and lived experience with the future of our country."

  
  IK
  
  In-Aam Kafaar
  61 Victoria, 2026
  
  
  
  
  
  
  "Reflecting on the first session during the Stellenbosch University Open Day, aside from the weather, I really enjoyed the experience, especially working with the Grade 12 learners, who were respectful and eager to engage, although both they and I were initially a bit nervous, which is understandable in a new environment. The second session, where we visited the school with my housemates, felt even more impactful, as many learners seemed more open to the idea of pursuing further education after Grade 12 and more comfortable discussing their future plans and possibilities. One of my biggest takeaways from this house project is how important exposure and access to information can be, especially since I also attended a public school with limited resources and often feel that an opportunity like this during my matric year would have helped me transition into university life more confidently by knowing what to expect. Even for those who may not choose the university route, it is important that they now know it is an available option, because sometimes opportunities feel limited simply due to lack of exposure, and learning about different paths can broaden horizons and help people imagine bigger futures. I was truly happy to be part of this project and grateful for the opportunity to contribute in a meaningful way"

  
  LM
  
  Lerato Mashedi
  61 Victoria, 2026
  
  
  
  
  
  
 

  
  WF
  
  Wayde Francis
  61 Victoria, 2026

  Collaboration & support
  Our Partners
  The organisations and individuals who have made this initiative possible through their support, resources, and collaboration.
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  Partners & supporters
  Who stands with us
  
  None of this work happens alone. These are the partners whose expertise,
resources, and belief in our mission have shaped every concept we have delivered.

  
  

        

          
            
              
                
              
              Makupula Secondary High School
            
            Our primary community partner. School leadership, teachers, and selected top-performing learners are central to every concept we deliver. Their openness and enthusiasm have made this project what it is.
          

          
            
              
                
              
              Centre for the Advancement of Social Impact and Transformation (CASIT)
            
            The Centre is mandated to advance, advocate, engage, advise, guide, develop, support, facilitate, activate, co-create, influence, enable, coordinate institutional engagements in terms of Social Impact and Transformation at Stellenbosch University.
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  Listen Live and Learn 
  
  The broader LLL cluster provides oversight, institutional continuity, and a community of practice that ensures the programme outlives any single house cohort and continues to grow year on year.
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  The Unidreams Foundation
  
  Unidreams Foundation is a youth-led nonprofit organization committed to bridging the gap between ambition and access for students from underserved communities. Founded on the belief that every dream is valid and achievable, we empower young people by providing mentorship, university application support, and resources to help them unlock their full academic and personal potential.
